14854.R1_justification: Participant discloses failing a course and lying to parents (didn't participate), and originates the protective motive 'I didn't want them to stress about my school and my motivation' (9:38) — self-reflective inner state, not just facts.
14854.R2_justification: Engages briefly with own reasoning/guilt around the lie rather than just naming an emotion, but stays guarded; deflects when AI pushes.
14854.disclosure_start: 9:37 'i have failed my last course... i lied to them'

16426.R1_justification: Beyond the factual missed deadline, participant originates inner reflection: 'I experienced how bad it feels it wont happen again (short-term). But I dont know if it will be a lasting effect' (9:32) — self-reflection on own behaviour.
16426.R2_justification: Reflects on the experience and its likely durability rather than just naming disappointment, though deflects ('dont know fam') when probed further.
16426.disclosure_start: 9:29 'I missed a deadline for an apllication'

14158.R1_justification: Describes a friend cutting contact after a semi-romantic relationship and originates self-reflection/regret: 'i was never decisive on what i wanted' and 'maybe should have never let the friendship develop into something romantic' (11:09-11:11).
14158.R2_justification: Reflects on own indecisiveness and regret over the relational consequence, beyond just naming facts.
14158.disclosure_start: 11:07 'i have a friend that doesnt want to talk to me anymore'

13507.R1_justification: Beyond describing the noisy housemate, participant originates inner state: 'I feel like I should have said something in the beginning... now it seems too late' and 'I find confrontation so tricky! I don't like complaining' (10:56, 10:58).
13507.R2_justification: Engages with own discomfort about confrontation rather than just naming it, revealing a small self-trait, but stays incident-focused.
13507.disclosure_start: 10:54 'My housemate is really getting on my nerves'

13444.R1_justification: Describes friend ending contact after a semi-romantic relationship; participant originates self-reflection/regret 'i was never decisive on what i wanted' and 'maybe should have never let the friendship develop into something romantic' (11:09-11:11).
13444.R2_justification: Reflects on own indecision and the relational fallout, exploring rather than just naming.

14293.R2_justification: Engages with the bodily/emotional stress reaction rather than just naming it, though it resolves quickly into smalltalk.

13627.R1_justification: Describes juggling uni/family/boyfriend; participant originates mild self-pressure inner state 'im stressing a bit to cover everything... without letting anyone behind' (11:40), but repeatedly minimizes ('nothing specific').
13627.R2_justification: Emotional tone (self-imposed pressure not to let people down) is named but only lightly engaged; she keeps deflecting into logistics.

14152.R1_justification: Describes friend's silent treatment over a trip and originates inner state and relational reflection: 'its so exhausting sometimes' and 'I dont know how close I want to keep that friendship' (12:03-12:04).
14152.R2_justification: Genuinely explores emotional exhaustion and ambivalence/reassessment of the friendship, beyond just naming the conflict.
14152.disclosure_start: 11:59 'one of them had a free day and proposed going on a day trip... my other friend got mad at me'

13351.R1_justification: Describes teammate dismissing her and originates inner state: frustration plus self-doubt 'this feeling is not neceserally objectively confirmed' and fear 'what if it al plays out bad' (12:27-12:29).
13351.R2_justification: Engages with own uncertainty about whether the gender-based perception is valid and anxiety about confronting it, not just naming frustration.
13351.disclosure_start: 12:04 'when I say something he kinda ignores it but when my male teammember says something he immidiatly listens... because of my gender'

13528.R1_justification: Factual account of a Vinted buyer cancelling; participant explicitly minimizes any inner state ('Pretty much a non problem', 'little negative impact on me', 12:11-12:14). No engaged inner world.
